explicit_text: I'm originally from Louisville, Kentucky, where I was born and raised. That's where my parents started mass producing ceramic pitcher and bowl sets back in the late 70s and early 80s. explicit_text: I started coming to Court Day in 1982 when I was 17, after a friend of my dad's, John Smith from Mount Sterling, invited me to join him. I set up on Queen Street, the same spot I use now, and have been coming every year since. Court Day was a friendly and welcoming event, and it became a big part of my selling calendar. It's where I met many people, including my future wife, and really grew my business over the decades.

explicit_text: Labor Day marks the start of my busy selling season, usually beginning around the first of September. I kick off the season at a big festival in Hillsville, Virginia, which lasts four days and draws about 2.5 million people.
